key_insight: The paper proposes an automated pipeline that turns complex symbolic algorithms into solvable recurrences using human-provided metrics/lemmas, higher-order abstract interpretation, and fixed-point-based bound verification.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce the manual effort and expertise needed to analyze the complexity of automated reasoning algorithms such as quantifier elimination in real and integer arithmetic.
method: User supplies key metrics and helpful lemmas to capture the creative parts of the proof.
Novel higher-order abstract interpretation extracts generalized recurrence equations from symbolic programs.
Recurrences are solved/verified via pre- and postfixpoint methods, supported by CAS and SMT techniques.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a theory and under-development tool, illustrated with examples from super-exponential quantifier elimination procedures, but does not report experimental results.
watch_item: The approach still depends on expert-chosen metrics and lemmas, so full automation may be limited by the need for domain knowledge and solver support.
paper_title: Towards an Automated Reasoning Tool for Complexity Analysis of Automated Reasoners
